posts.title: 🏫 Yearbook Update - PVHS
posts.summary: Yearbook sales and distribution begin May 27th. Go to the Business Office window to purchase, or room 431 if you pre-purchased.
posts.author: Daniel Dearborn
posts.body:
Yearbook Update

Sales and distribution will begin May 27th.

On May 27th:

  • If you want to purchase a yearbook, go to the Business Office window.
  • If you already pre-purchased a yearbook, go to room 431.

posts.title: 📬 PV Weekly Admin Update 4/17/2026
posts.summary: Weekly PVHS newsletter: Erica Alfaro presentation recap, Link Crew, Fiddler on the Roof, CAASPP Science testing, Unified Track, CSF recognition, PSAT, and AHC Launch to College.
posts.author: Araceli Mandujano-Padilla
posts.body:
WEEKLY UPDATE - PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL - April 17, 2026

Hello Panthers. Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter.

Motivational speaker Erica Alfaro Presents at PVHS

PVHS, in partnership with TKJHS, offered our School Community a chance to hear an inspirational message from Erica Alfaro, an award-winning author, keynote speaker, and educational advocate. Thank you to Erica and to Maria Garcia and John Calandro from TKJHS for partnering with us.

Link Crew

Link Crew Advisors met this week to review Link Crew Leader applications. Link Crew is a year-long transition program to help our 9th graders through orientation, academic follow-ups, social follow-ups, and leader-initiated contacts.

🎭 Fiddler on the Roof at PVHS

Friday and Saturday at Pioneer Valley High School. Our Drama and Music students take the stage for Fiddler on the Roof. Thank you to Drama Director Selyn Harwin, Band Director Lisbet Barriga, and Scott Wonnell for set construction.

🧪 CAASPP Testing Update

CAASPP testing is underway in the Science window. Testing continues Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday of next week.

Unified Track: Panther Pride

A huge shout-out to everyone who supported our Unified Athletes. Special thanks to Coach Ben Alberry. Unified Sports brings together students of all abilities to compete on the same team.

CSF Honor Student Recognition

PV had 57 of its 78 CSF seal bearers attend the Kiwanis Club of Santa Maria's 64th annual CSF Honor Student Recognition dinner on Wednesday, April 15th.

Progress 2 and Important Dates

  • Progress 2 for Spring grades ends Friday, April 24th (minimum day).
  • Wednesday, April 22nd: PSAT in the library for registered sophomores. Check-in 7:30am.
  • Tuesday, April 21st: Allan Hancock Launch to College Day for registered seniors.

Medical Careers: Doctors In The House

Mrs. Martinez's Medical Careers class had a hands-on suturing lab supported by Dr. Rehder, Dr. Lirgg, and Dr. Golub, as part of their unit on the integumentary system.

posts.title: 🎓 Senior Information - 4/16/26
posts.summary: Senior updates: Josten's cap/gown orders April 28 during lunch, Prom tickets on sale until April 30, Grad Nite June 3, Graduation Practice June 10 at 1pm.
posts.author: Lisa Walters
posts.body:
Good morning!

Josten's will be on campus DURING LUNCH ONLY on April 28 to take the last of the cap and gown orders ($65). You can also order at jostens.com. If you ordered but weren't here to pick up on April 2, go to the administration office.

All seniors must have a walking partner - one will wear black and the other will wear teal. Exchanges can be made on April 28.

Prom tickets are on sale until April 30 ($65). Students need a Prom contract. Guests require a copy of their ID and an approved guest pass. Prom dates must be no older than 20 years old per SMJUHSD policy. Tickets available at Business Office 8:00-4:00.

Grad Nite will be on June 3. Tickets were pre-sold and are sold out. Seniors meet in the student parking lot no later than 5:15 am. Buses leave at 5:30 am. We return June 4 around 8 am.

Seniors: wear closed-toed shoes, wear pants (not shorts - gets cold), take a jacket, bring a pillow and blankie for the bus. You can leave items on the buses (they stay parked and locked). Take makeup/perfume in a separate bag to leave on the bus.

Note: If stopped by Disneyland police and taken to Disney jail, we'll call your parents immediately to come pick you up. That is non-negotiable.

Graduation Practice begins at 1 pm on June 10 IN THE GYM. All seniors report there after the last final. Sit with your walking partner. Ends at 3 pm.

Questions? Contact Lisa Walters at lwalters@smjuhsd.org.

posts.title: 📝 CAASPP state testing for all juniors begins tomorrow
posts.summary: CAASPP state testing for all juniors begins tomorrow. Make sure students get a good night's sleep, arrive on time, and bring their fully charged school-issued tablet.
posts.author: Merrie Okie
posts.body:
Dear Students, Staff, and Families,

This is a reminder that CAASPP state testing for all juniors begins tomorrow.

Please help set students up for success by making sure they:

  • Get a good night's sleep
  • Arrive on time
  • Bring their fully charged school-issued tablet

We encourage all students to take the CAASPP seriously and do their best. These assessments help us understand how students are developing essential skills in reading, writing, and mathematics, and provide valuable information for schools and the state.

Thank you for your continued support.

Sincerely,
Merrie Goldin
Assistant Principal of Special Programs
Pioneer Valley High School
805-922-1305 ext. 5707
mgoldin@smjuhsd.org

posts.title: 🎓 Get Ahead This Summer! Allan Hancock Enrollment Workshop
posts.summary: Summer college credits workshop for Allan Hancock College registration. Monday, April 13 during lunch in the School Library. Students must bring their charged school laptop.
posts.author: Israel Hernandez
posts.body:
🚀 Summer College Credits: Allan Hancock Enrollment Workshop

Help your student get ahead this summer. We are hosting a dedicated workshop to walk students through the Allan Hancock College summer registration process. Whether they want to earn college credit early or explore a new subject, this is the perfect time to start.

The Details

  • 📅 When: Monday, April 13
  • ⏰ Time: During Lunch
  • 📍 Where: School Library
  • 💻 Requirement: Students must bring their charged school laptop

Don't let your student miss out. See you in the library this Monday at lunch.

posts.title: 🎤 Please join PVHS & TKJHS for a Motivational Keynote Presentation from Erica Alfaro 4/13/26 @ 6PM in the PVHS Library
posts.summary: Motivational speaker Erica Alfaro will present at PVHS on Monday 4/13/26 at 6pm in the PVHS Library. Award-winning author, keynote speaker, and educational advocate.
posts.author: Paul Robinson
posts.body:
Motivational speaker Erica Alfaro will be presenting at PVHS on Monday 4/13/26 at 6pm in the PVHS Library.

PVHS, in partnership with TKJHS, is very excited to offer our School Community a chance to hear an inspirational message from Erica Alfaro. Erica is an award-winning author, keynote speaker, and educational advocate.

In 2019, Erica's graduation pictures went viral and made headlines around the world. She was featured in major media outlets such as CNN, The Huffington Post, and Yahoo News.

Erica's Harvesting Dreams book is an empowering, inspirational and motivational story. It is a testimony of a girl who went through many adversities and managed to reinvent herself. Erica's book won the 2022 International Latino Book Award in the Most Inspirational Young Adult category.

Don't miss this wonderful opportunity to be inspired.

posts.title: 📬 PVHS Weekly Newsletter 4/2/2026
posts.summary: Weekly PVHS newsletter: Spring Break 4/3-4/10, Erica Alfaro guest speaker 4/13, Rotary essay winner, CAASPP testing begins 4/16, Senior Event, Junior Financial Aid, Spring Faire, BLE Cycle Assessment.
posts.author: Araceli Mandujano-Padilla
posts.body:
WEEKLY UPDATE - PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL - April 2, 2026

Hello Panthers. Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter.

Spring Break

Spring Break is here. There will be no classes from Friday 4/3/26 to Friday 4/10/26. We return on Monday 4/13/26. Wishing all students and staff a well-deserved time to rest, recharge, and enjoy the season.

Guest Speaker 4/13

Motivational speaker Erica Alfaro will be presenting at PVHS on Monday 4/13/26 at 6pm in the PVHS Library. Award-winning author, keynote speaker, and educational advocate.

Rotary Club Essay Contest

Congratulations to Zaira Nacar for submitting a winning essay in the Santa Maria Breakfast Rotary's 4-Way Test Essay Contest. She will read her winning essay to the Rotary group on April 30th.

CAASPP Testing Begins April 16

CAASPP state testing window begins with Science (CAST) on April 16. Strong performance on 11th-grade CAASPP English and Math may be used by California community colleges and CSU campuses for course placement.

  • Get a good night's sleep
  • Eat breakfast
  • Take their time
  • Show what they know

Senior Event

Seniors gathered for a senior meeting, captured memories with a panoramic picture, and received graduation gowns, all hosted by Mrs. Walters.

Junior Financial Aid Presentations

Counselors met with all 11th grade students this week to highlight the importance of applying for financial aid.

Spring Faire

Annual Spring Faire where our clubs came together to sell items and fundraise for their organizations.

BLE Cycle Assessment

The Biology of the Living Earth (BLE) team from all three comprehensive sites came together to begin creating cycle assessments using Inner Orbit and Gemini platforms.

Have a wonderful and restful Spring Break, Panthers!

posts.title: 🎓 Meet with an Allan Hancock College Counselor at PVHS Tuesday, April 21st
posts.summary: Seniors planning to attend Allan Hancock College in the fall should scan the QR code to reserve an appointment time with an AHC Counselor on Tuesday, April 21st.
posts.author: Melani Mederos
posts.body:
Hey Panthers!

If you're planning on attending Allan Hancock College in the fall, and you haven't met with an AHC Counselor yet, scan the QR code to reserve your appointment time for Tuesday, April 21st.

You must meet with an AHC Counselor to get your "First Semester Plan" so you can register for classes in May.

Questions? Please see your PVHS Counselor, Mr. Hernandez in the library, or Mrs. Mederos in the College & Career Center (room 604).
